title: Summer meadow linen spray
subtitle: A light floral linen spray with lavender, chamomile, and rose geranium essential oils
excerpt: A linen spray is a dilute, alcohol-based fragrance misted onto bedding, pillowcases, and soft furnishings. This blend uses lavender, Roman chamomile, and rose geranium: soft, floral, and calming. The alcohol evaporates within seconds, leaving only the fragrance on the fabric. A small amount of distilled water prevents the spray from drying too fast.
sourceNotes: Linen spray formulation. Isopropyl alcohol 99% at 25 to 30%: primary carrier, disperses EOs in water, evaporates cleanly from fabric. Distilled water at 68 to 73%. Essential oils at 2%: lavender (Lavandula angustifolia), Roman chamomile (Anthemis nobilis), rose geranium (Pelargonium graveolens). All within IFRA fabric application limits. Shake before each use.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep IPA at 25 to 30% for clean evaporation from fabric.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 6 months in a sealed spray bottle. Floral top notes fade over time; lavender persists longest. Keep away from direct sunlight.

glossaryTerms.definition: A light alcohol and water solution carrying fragrance, designed to be misted onto fabric. The alcohol evaporates within seconds, carrying the fragrance into the fibres and leaving no damp residue on dry fabric.

Isopropyl or ethyl alcohol in a linen spray serves two purposes: it disperses essential oils in water, and its quick evaporation drives the fragrance molecules into the fabric fibres rather than letting them evaporate into the air before contact.

content.text: is simpler to make than a room spray and more targeted: the fragrance goes directly onto the fabric rather than into the air.

keeps the oils dispersed and ensures fast evaporation so the bedding does not feel damp. Lavender and chamomile make a soft combination for a bedroom spray. Rose geranium adds a slightly rosier dimension. Isopropyl alcohol is flammable; keep the bottle away from open flames.

items.prepNote: Distilled or freshly boiled and cooled

content.text: Add isopropyl alcohol to the spray bottle. Add essential oils to the alcohol and swirl to mix. Add the distilled water. Cap and shake for 30 seconds. Label with the date and contents.
Shake before every use. Hold about 30 cm above the fabric and mist lightly. Allow to dry before contact with skin. Do not over-spray; 2 to 3 spritzes per pillowcase is sufficient.
6 months in a sealed spray bottle away from sunlight. Shake before each use.
